hazards.title: CUDA Core Segmentation Faults
hazards.description: Custom compiled C++/CUDA layers directly manipulate GPU core register allocations. Out-of-bounds pointer allocations trigger immediate container hardware faults.
hazards.mitigation_protocol: Pre-validate safety allocations within the host CPU sandbox, implement strict memory size boundary checks prior to GPU pointer conversion, and leverage TensorRT native runtime protection boundaries.

hazards.title: VRAM Allocation Engine Build OOM
hazards.description: Shedding model weights during quantization and compilation stages demands severe VRAM resources. If executed on lightweight developer platforms, the build agent process will be forcefully terminated by the OS Out-Of-Memory (OOM) killer.
hazards.mitigation_protocol: Execute compilation pipelines exclusively on massive isolated multi-GPU build hosts (i.e. Cloud Build specialized machine categories with dedicated host limits) rather than standard sandboxed developer containers.

hazards.title: C++ ABI Version Mismatches
hazards.description: Linking pre-compiled binary modules or custom extensions with divergent compiler systems can trigger C++ Application Binary Interface (ABI) incompatibilities.
hazards.mitigation_protocol: Strictly utilize the exact same compiler version (GCC/G++ matching Ubuntu version specs) across development builds, dockerized environments, and serverless runtime targets.

hazards.title: Concurrent Input Queues Deadlocking
hazards.description: Managing streaming inference queues through multi-threaded loop structures can lead to asynchronous race conditions and locked execution pipelines.
hazards.mitigation_protocol: Adopt rigid thread-safe lock pools, isolate inbound routing threads via asyncio.Queue structures, and enforce clear query task bounds timeouts across all API gateways.
